dashboard.label: T1041 â€” Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
dashboard.description: Chained detection â€” fires when a beaconing (src, dest) pair (from the T1071.001 rule) also moves >= 100 KB uplink. Risk-accumulation across two signals.

search.query: `c2_exfil` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | stats sum(total_orig_bytes) AS bytes | eval bytes_human = round(bytes / 1024, 1) . " KB" | table bytes_human

`c2_exfil` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | eval bytes_kb=round(total_orig_bytes/1024, 1) | sort -total_orig_bytes | table window, src, dest, bytes_kb, exfil_record_count, beacon_connection_count, beacon_avg_interval_seconds

panel.title: Beacon vs exfil â€” both signals over time

search.query: index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:conn | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | timechart span=15m count AS beacon_conns, sum(eval(if(orig_bytes >= 10000, orig_bytes, 0))) AS uplink_bytes

html.h3: T1041 â€” Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
html.p: Operators reuse the established C2 connection to push files, credentials, and database exports â€” the destination is already allow-listed because the beacon's been talking for hours. The detection composes two signals: beaconing (the T1071.001 rule's output) and large uplink bytes on the same connection.

Splunk does composition via the join type=inner subsearch in the macro. The "risk accumulation" pattern this implements is what Splunk Enterprise Security shops use in practice: behavioural + volumetric = high-confidence alert.
